# Model Report — Rice Yield Prediction (Sierra Leone)

*Target:* **Rice_Yield** (kg/ha) · *Source:* FAOSTAT 2000–2024 ·
*Generated by* `src/run_pipeline.py`

## 1. Design

- **Data.** FAOSTAT long-format CSV pivoted to one row per year, columns =
  crop × element. Only the **nine complete crops** (Rice, Cassava, Maize,
  Groundnuts, Oil palm fruit, Sweet potatoes, Sorghum, Cocoa beans, Plantains)
  and three elements (Production, Area harvested, Yield) are used. Yams,
  aggregates (`… primary`, `… n.e.c.`) and processed products are excluded.
- **Predictors (35).** lag-1 of every crop × element column;
  rice-yield lags 1–3; past-only 3- and 5-year rolling means of rice yield;
  linear year trend; Ebola (2014–16), COVID (2020–21) and Feed Salone (2023+)
  dummies.
- **Validation.** Expanding-window walk-forward only — train on all years before
  the test year, predict that one year, for 2018–2024
  (7 out-of-sample predictions). No random splits.
- **Models.** XGBoost (depth 2), GradientBoosting (depth 2), RandomForest
  (depth 3) — all shallow/regularized, fixed settings, no grid search — plus two
  baselines: persistence (last year's yield) and the 3-year rolling mean.

## 2. Anti-leakage rules applied

1. **No same-year features.** Every predictor for year *t* is knowable before
   *t* begins. Cross-crop signals enter only as their *t-1* value.
2. **Rolling means use past years only** — `shift(1)` is applied *before*
   `rolling()`, so year *t*'s window never includes year *t*.
3. **No rice production-per-hectare feature** — that reconstructs the target.
4. A runtime assertion in `features.py` fails the build if any bare same-year
   crop column appears among the predictors.

## 3. Results (walk-forward, 2018–2024)

| Model                       |     R2 |   RMSE |   MAE |
|:----------------------------|-------:|-------:|------:|
| Baseline: persistence (t-1) | -0.039 |  427.8 | 341.3 |
| RandomForest                | -0.247 |  468.5 | 454.7 |
| Baseline: 3-yr rolling mean | -0.565 |  524.9 | 511.4 |
| XGBoost                     | -0.591 |  529.4 | 497.2 |
| GradientBoosting            | -0.630 |  535.8 | 496.7 |

*Metrics computed across the 7 held-out yearly predictions.*

## 4. Verdict

**No ML model beats the persistence baseline.** The best ML model (RandomForest) records RMSE 468.5 kg/ha versus 427.8 kg/ha for simply predicting last year's yield. On a 25-year national series this is the expected, honest outcome: with ~13–18 training rows there is not enough signal for a tree ensemble to out-predict the strong autocorrelation that 'last year' already captures. Overall lowest RMSE across all five contenders: **Baseline: persistence (t-1)** (RMSE 427.8, R² -0.039, MAE 341.3). Per the design rules, models were **not** tuned and features were **not** added to chase a higher R². R² across only 7 points is high-variance and can be negative; RMSE/MAE in kg/ha are the more interpretable metrics here.

## 5. Explainability

Feature importance and SHAP summary plots (`outputs/figures/`) are produced for
the best ML model (**RandomForest**), refit on the full leakage-free dataset
(2005–2024) *after* evaluation — for interpretation
only, never for model selection.
